get.description:
The board a new game would open on, for the map picker — no game is
created. Terrain and ports depend only on the seed (not the placement).

post.description:
Create a game, or return the caller's place in line when the server
is at its concurrency cap (a ``202`` they re-POST with ``ticket``).

schema.title: Response Post Create Api Games Post

post.description: Apply the acting seat's move; the request must prove that seat.

get.description:
Server-sent events: the requester's snapshot now, then again on
every state change (moves, bot plays, chat, joins). Comment lines
keep idle connections alive.

post.description: Append a chat message to the game log.

get.description:
The finished game as ``GameRecord`` JSON -- a self-contained,
replayable transcript (served for past games too, rebuilt from the
store). 409 while running; 404 if unknown.

post.description:
Load a finished game for replay (a past game too, rebuilt from the
store). 409 while it is still running; 404 if unknown.

get.description:
The loaded replay after ``move`` moves (0 = the opening board).

``404`` when no replay is loaded; ``422`` when ``move`` is out of
range.
The loaded replay's record JSON (e.g. to save it to a file).

Bot kinds available for seats — one per registered bot service — each
with its title, description, and the player counts it supports.

schema.title: Response Get Bots Api Bots Get
Response List Bot Providers Api Admin Bot Providers Get

post.description:
Register (or replace) a remote bot service by base URL; the bot it
serves joins the catalog under its own name. ``400`` if it is unreachable
(admin only).

schema.title: Response Register Bot Provider Api Admin Bot Providers Post

delete.description: Unregister a remote bot provider (admin only); ``404`` if unknown.

get.description:
The signed-in user's games — those still live where their account
owns a seat — so they can resume on any device without a seat token.

schema.title: Response My Games Api Me Games Get

get.description:
The signed-in user's finished games (newest first) — their seats, the
winner, and the player count — each replayable / downloadable by id.

schema.title: Response My History Api Me History Get

LOGIN_BAD_CREDENTIALS.summary: Bad credentials or the user is inactive.

REGISTER_USER_ALREADY_EXISTS.summary: A user with this email already exists.

UPDATE_USER_EMAIL_ALREADY_EXISTS.summary: A user with this email already exists.
A user with this email already exists.

ActionModel.title: ActionModel
ActionModel.description:
One legal move for the acting player.

``flat`` is the game's flat action index (post it back to apply the
move). ``type`` is the lowercased action type. Depending on the type, at
most one geometry/resource group below is populated; the rest stay ``None``.

BankModel.title: BankModel
BankModel.description: Cards left in the supply: resource stacks plus the development deck.

BeliefModel.title: BeliefModel
BeliefModel.description:
Card counting from one human seat's perspective.

Everything here is derivable from public information (the card-counting
tracker), so showing it to that seat never leaks hidden state. The
observer's own row is omitted — their hand is already on screen.

BotMoveModel.title: BotMoveModel
BotMoveModel.description: One just-played bot move: the seat that acted and the decoded action.

BuildingModel.title: BuildingModel
BuildingModel.description: A settlement or city sitting on a vertex, owned by a 0-indexed player.

CubeModel.title: CubeModel
CubeModel.description: A board vertex, in cube coordinates (q + r + s = ±1).

DevCardCounts.title: DevCardCounts
DevCardCounts.description: Unplayed development cards in hand, by type (DevCard order).

EdgeModel.title: EdgeModel
EdgeModel.description: A board edge between two vertices (in cube coordinates).

GameModel.title: GameModel
GameModel.description:
One requester's snapshot: board + status + their legal moves.

Views are per-seat: ``your_turn`` / ``actions`` / unredacted hands /
``belief`` follow the seats the request's tokens prove.

GameStatusModel.title: GameStatusModel
GameStatusModel.description: Turn-flow snapshot for the live game.

LogEntryModel.title: LogEntryModel
LogEntryModel.description:
One line of the game's chat / log.

``player`` is the seat the line belongs to (``None``: a spectator's chat
message). Move entries carry the ``action_type`` (the client maps it to an
icon) and a short text (the action label, or ``"rolled N"`` for rolls).

PlayerBeliefModel.title: PlayerBeliefModel
PlayerBeliefModel.description: The observer's proven bounds on one player's hand (``lo == hi``: exact).

PlayerModel.title: PlayerModel
PlayerModel.description:
Summary stats for one (0-indexed) player.

The per-type breakdowns are private: the server nulls them out for every
seat the requester doesn't own (counts stay public, as at a real table).

PortModel.title: PortModel
PortModel.description:
A harbour spanning two coastal vertices.

``resource`` is the resource of a 2:1 port, or ``None`` for a 3:1 general
port.

ReplayStateModel.title: ReplayStateModel
ReplayStateModel.description:
The Replay view's snapshot after ``move`` of ``n_moves`` moves.

``log`` holds the moves played up to that point (the win line appears only
at the final move); ``winner`` / ``seats`` describe the whole record.

ResourceCounts.title: ResourceCounts
ResourceCounts.description: Resource cards in hand, by type (Tile resource order).

RoadModel.title: RoadModel
RoadModel.description: A road along an edge between two vertices, owned by a 0-indexed player.

TradeOfferModel.title: TradeOfferModel
TradeOfferModel.description: The pending 1:1 domestic trade awaiting the partner's answer.

_CreatedModel.title: _CreatedModel
_CreatedModel.description: A fresh game: its id and the creator's seat tokens.

_MyGameModel.title: _MyGameModel
_MyGameModel.description: A live game the signed-in user owns a seat in.

_PastGameModel.title: _PastGameModel
_PastGameModel.description: A finished game in the user's history.

_ProviderRequest.title: _ProviderRequest
_ProviderRequest.description: A remote bot service to register: its base URL (the bot self-identifies).

_QueuedModel.title: _QueuedModel
_QueuedModel.description:
The server is at its concurrency cap: the caller's place in line. They
re-POST with ``ticket`` until they get a :class:`_CreatedModel` back.
